description: Golden identifier set the automated pipeline should retrieve/resolve for the image-based skin-lesion malignancy / biopsy-referral decision-support reference case (Case 6). Companion to the HRV/DR/warfarin/sepsis/AFib goldens. This is a DEVICE case (intervention_type=device) and is regulatory-POSITIVE (three adjunctive Class II product codes exist - QZS via De Novo DEN230008, plus OYD/ONV under 21 CFR 878.1820 with PMAs P090012/P150046) carrying a regulatory-NULL TWIST on the MODALITY: every FDA-authorized device in this space uses a different physical signal (QZS=elastic-scattering spectroscopy, OYD=multispectral optical imaging, ONV=electrical impedance spectroscopy); there is NO authorization for a device whose input is dermoscopic/clinical PHOTOGRAPHS analyzed by image AI - the exact system under evaluation. This is the slate's FIRST case with PMA numbers (P090012 MelaFind, P150046 Nevisense). Use in the ablation harness: score recall = golden ids present in the bundle's records (FDA codes / DEN / PMA / NCT EXACT; literature as a >=K resolvable-ID floor per COMPARISON.md Lesson 2), precision = on-topic rate. Every id in 'required' resolved live 2026-07-09 (PMIDs via E-utils esummary, DOIs via Crossref, product codes via openFDA device/classification, DEN via openFDA device/510k, PMA via openFDA device/pma, NCTs via ClinicalTrials.gov v2).
reference_case: Image-based skin-lesion malignancy risk / biopsy-referral decision support: a photograph-in / malignancy-risk-out AI classifier of dermoscopic or clinical skin-lesion images, providing an adjunctive second-read biopsy/refer recommendation to a physician not specialized in skin-cancer diagnosis. Non-standalone decision support; the pivotal endpoint is the change in the human biopsy/refer decision, not the model's isolated AUC.
mesh_note: Condition authored condition-forward ('skin-lesion malignancy / melanoma'), so Case-4 Finding A cannot bite. 'Melanoma' (MeSH D008545) sits under the broader 'Skin Neoplasms' (D012878); whether the '+hierarchy' axis diverges here is determined empirically by the sweep and written up in ablation_findings.md - not asserted in advance.

match_policy.required_min_coverage: All ids in 'required' should be present for full recall. 'field_grounding' is the per-field breakdown following each source's role in the spec's SOURCE INVENTORY. Like DR/warfarin/sepsis/AFib, melanoma has a scored 'ncts' category, but ONLY the 2 load-bearing trials (NCT06690086 sample-size anchor; NCT06666790 postmarket-monitoring template). The 2 additional NCTs the spec mentions (NCT06080711, NCT03362138) are explicitly 'not used as numeric anchors' - design-norm context only - and are recorded in 'context_only_identifiers' (NOT scored). This resolves the 4-vs-2 NCT count: 4 mentioned, 2 load-bearing/scored. Melanoma introduces 'fda_pma_numbers' (first case with PMAs); it has no 'fda_k_numbers' (no 510(k) predicate in the same modality) and no 'fda_nda_numbers' (device case, no drug).
match_policy.resolution_pass: PMID -> E-utils esummary; DOI -> Crossref works; product_code -> openFDA device/classification; DEN (De Novo) -> openFDA device/510k (De Novo grants indexed with a DEN k_number - verified live: DEN230008 returns DermaSensor/QZS); PMA -> openFDA device/pma (verified live: P090012 MelaFind, P150046 Nevisense); NCT -> ClinicalTrials.gov v2. A required id that fails to resolve is a TEST FAILURE, not a silent drop.
match_policy.regulatory_positive_with_modality_null: POSITIVE: three adjunctive Class II product codes are FDA-authorized - QZS (21 CFR 878.1830, created by De Novo DEN230008, DermaSensor 2024-01-12), OYD (21 CFR 878.1820, MelaFind multispectral optical imaging, PMA P090012), ONV (21 CFR 878.1820, Nevisense electrical impedance spectroscopy, PMA P150046). MODALITY NULL TWIST: searching openFDA 510(k)/De Novo/PMA for dermoscopic/photographic image-analysis skin devices returns NO authorization for a device whose input is a photograph analyzed by image AI - every cleared device uses a DIFFERENT physical signal. A photograph-in image-AI classifier of the type described has no same-modality authorized predicate, which pushes it toward De Novo and makes signal-validation (Field 2) and benchmarks (Field 3) pivotal.

context_only_identifiers.description: Identifiers cited in the melanoma spec for context but deliberately NOT scored. The harness reads only the 'required' block (ablation_harness.py:256), so nothing here is counted as a hit or a miss. Recorded for a complete audit trail.

ncts.reason: Surfaced by broader AI-dermatology searches confirming the reader/decision-study design norm for Field 1; the spec states it was 'not used as numeric anchors'. Resolves live but is not a load-bearing citation, so scoring it would penalize correct pipeline behavior.
Same as NCT06080711 - a design-norm exemplar, not a numeric anchor. Resolves live; recorded here for provenance, not scored.

expected_conclusions.regulatory_class: Class II, product code QZS (21 CFR 878.1830), via De Novo (predicate DEN230008) or 510(k) to the QZS predicate; related adjunctive codes OYD (P090012) and ONV (P150046) are Class II under 878.1820.

regulatory_null_evidence.claim: No FDA authorization exists for a device that estimates skin-lesion malignancy by AI analysis of dermoscopic/clinical PHOTOGRAPHS. Every FDA-authorized device in this space uses a different physical signal (QZS elastic-scattering spectroscopy / OYD multispectral optical imaging / ONV electrical impedance spectroscopy).
regulatory_null_evidence.how_verified: openFDA 510(k), De Novo and PMA endpoints searched for dermoscop*/photograph*/photographic image-analysis skin devices this session; returned no same-modality photograph-input image-AI classifier. The positive codes QZS/OYD/ONV and grants DEN230008/P090012/P150046 are the adjacent-but-different-modality landscape, all scored in 'required'.

coverage_gaps_expected: Full-text pivotal DermaSensor / MelaFind / Nevisense clinical studies (device-specific sensitivity/specificity numbers not asserted)
FDA De Novo decision summary / 510(k) summary PDFs (openFDA summary text field empty for DEN230008)
March 2026 Federal Register reclassification rule text (doc 2026-05772; Class II status of OYD/ONV taken from openFDA classification fields, not the rule)
Drugs@FDA / drug labeling (not applicable - software/imaging device, not a drug or biologic)
